content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes two numbers and an operator (+, -, *, /) as input and performs the corresponding operation using a switch statement. Print the result upto 2 decimal places.
constraints: Division by zero will never occur
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two space‑separated numbers num1 and num2. The second line contains a single character operator which is one of '+', '-', '*', '/'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the result of applying the operator to num1 and num2, rounded to exactly two decimal places (e.g., 3.00, -1.50).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only one test case. 1 + 2 = 3, printed with two decimal places.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each case read the two operands as doubles and the operator character. Use a switch statement to perform the required arithmetic operation. Print the result using a format that forces exactly two digits after the decimal point. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0].strip())
    idx = 1
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        num1, num2 = map(float, data[idx].split())
        idx += 1
        op = data[idx].strip()
        idx += 1
        if op == '+':
            res = num1 + num2
        elif op == '-':
            res = num1 - num2
        elif op == '*':
            res = num1 * num2
        else:  # '/'
            res = num1 / num2
        out.append(f"{res:.2f}")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
